messages.message:
Error: Calling setState synchronously within an effect can trigger cascading renders

Effects are intended to synchronize state between React and external systems such as manually updating the DOM, state management libraries, or other platform APIs. In general, the body of an effect should do one or both of the following:
* Update external systems with the latest state from React.
* Subscribe for updates from some external system, calling setState in a callback function when external state changes.

Calling setState synchronously within an effect body causes cascading renders that can hurt performance, and is not recommended. (https://react.dev/learn/you-might-not-need-an-effect).

/home/doodcom/Documents/Vortex Agentic V2/src/components/AssistantView.tsx:78:12
  76 |   useEffect(() => {
  77 |     const q = consumeTerminalQuery()
> 78 |     if (q) setInput(q)
     |            ^^^^^^^^ Avoid calling setState() directly within an effect
  79 |   }, [])
  80 |   const [isDiagnosing, setIsDiagnosing] = useState(false)
  81 |   const [project, setProject] = useState<{ path: string, fileCount: number } | null>(null)
Unexpected any. Specify a different type.

suggestions.desc: Use `unknown` instead, this will force you to explicitly, and safely assert the type is correct.
Use `never` instead, this is useful when instantiating generic type parameters that you don't need to know the type of.

messages.message:
Error: Calling setState synchronously within an effect can trigger cascading renders

Effects are intended to synchronize state between React and external systems such as manually updating the DOM, state management libraries, or other platform APIs. In general, the body of an effect should do one or both of the following:
* Update external systems with the latest state from React.
* Subscribe for updates from some external system, calling setState in a callback function when external state changes.

Calling setState synchronously within an effect body causes cascading renders that can hurt performance, and is not recommended. (https://react.dev/learn/you-might-not-need-an-effect).

/home/doodcom/Documents/Vortex Agentic V2/src/components/DashboardView.tsx:135:5
  133 |     const ram = stats.memory ? (stats.memory.used / stats.memory.total) * 100 : 0
  134 |     const gpu = stats.gpu?.utilizationGpu ?? 0
> 135 |     setCpuHist(prev => [...prev, cpu].slice(-40))
      |     ^^^^^^^^^^ Avoid calling setState() directly within an effect
  136 |     setRamHist(prev => [...prev, ram].slice(-40))
  137 |     setGpuHist(prev => [...prev, gpu].slice(-40))
  138 |   }, [stats])

Error: Cannot access variable before it is declared

`checkHaConnection` is accessed before it is declared, which prevents the earlier access from updating when this value changes over time.

/home/doodcom/Documents/Vortex Agentic V2/src/components/HomeView.tsx:31:5
  29 |     localStorage.setItem('vortex-ha-ip', haIp)
  30 |     localStorage.setItem('vortex-ha-token', haToken)
> 31 |     checkHaConnection()
     |     ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ `checkHaConnection` accessed before it is declared
  32 |   }, [haIp, haToken])
  33 |
  34 |   const fetchCameraPreview = async (entityId: string) => {

/home/doodcom/Documents/Vortex Agentic V2/src/components/HomeView.tsx:49:3
  47 |   }
  48 |
> 49 |   const checkHaConnection = async () => {
     |   ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
> 50 |     if (!haIp) return
     | ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
> 51 |     try {
     …
     | ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
> 67 |     }
     | ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
> 68 |   }
     | ^^^^ `checkHaConnection` is declared here
  69 |
  70 |   const fetchEntities = async () => {
  71 |     if (!haToken) return
React Hook useEffect has a missing dependency: 'checkHaConnection'. Either include it or remove the dependency array.

suggestions.desc: Update the dependencies array to be: [checkHaConnection, haIp, haToken]

messages.message: 'e' is defined but never used.

React Hook useEffect has missing dependencies: 'liveRefreshRef' and 'setLiveRefreshKey'. Either include them or remove the dependency array. If 'setLiveRefreshKey' changes too often, find the parent component that defines it and wrap that definition in useCallback.

suggestions.desc: Update the dependencies array to be: [autoRefresh, liveRefreshRef, setLiveRefreshKey]

messages.message: Fast refresh only works when a file only exports components. Use a new file to share constants or functions between components.
Fast refresh only works when a file only exports components. Use a new file to share constants or functions between components.
Unexpected constant nullishness on the left-hand side of a `??` expression.
